title: Docker Architecture, OCI Boundaries, Isolation, and Environment Evidence · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Version Context Baseline
summary: A civic data team must decide whether containers solve its reproducibility problem without claiming virtual-machine isolation or ignoring daemon authority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker version context baseline increment in container architecture and environment evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state.
Compare Docker Engine and Docker Desktop host, VM, filesystem, networking, licensing, and Linux-versus-Windows container boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Version Context Baseline · Theory Docker Version Context Baseline
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Version Context Baseline contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Version Context Baseline · Theory Docker Version Context Baseline.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture client, server, api, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing docker state. For Predict Docker Version Context Baseline · Theory Docker Version Context Baseline, keep Docker Version Context Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Docker Version Context Baseline while producing container architecture and environment evidence map.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine. Competency probe: Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state. A defensible solution applies Docker Version Context Baseline, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to container architecture and environment evidence map.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-version-context-baseline — Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-architecture-isolation-evidence-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-architecture-isolation-evidence-3
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked docker architecture isolation evidence 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      operation: pull
      path: client -> engine-api -> daemon -> containerd -> registry
      kernel-boundary: shared-host-kernel
    verify: docker context inspect training && docker info --format json
    failure: Reject any conclusion based only on the client version or a claim that the container boots a guest kernel.
    repair: Capture client and server versions, active context, platform, runtime, storage driver, and security options separately.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A docker --version string proves the client is connected to the intended compatible daemon. This fails the Docker Version Context Baseline contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-version-context-baseline-xqadkd
  - competency: docker-version-context-baseline
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: version context baseline
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: effective user capability filesystem and network inspection
    case-key: xqadkd
    change: |
      operation: pull
      path: client -> engine-api -> daemon -> containerd -> registry
      kernel-boundary: shared-host-kernel
    verify: docker context inspect training && docker info --format json
    failure: Reject any conclusion based only on the client version or a claim that the container boots a guest kernel.
    repair: Capture client and server versions, active context, platform, runtime, storage driver, and security options separately.
